hypothesis: Replacing the fixed (d=512) linear projection bottleneck between the frozen LLM encoder and the trainable decoder with a learned cross-attention adapter that dynamically routes encoder representations according to constraint-type (material inclusion/exclusion, adjacency, thickness bounds, angle) will reduce the validation ∆E gap relative to training-paraphrase performance by at least 30%, because constraint-specific routing will allow the decoder to selectively amplify the latent dimensions most predictive of each constraint class rather than compressing all linguistic information into a single undifferentiated fixed-dimensional vector.
